serving.description: 1 serving as sold (estimated ~300 g combined beverage and/or cake portion)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard café serving of Shouka Sunrise as sold at EC Coffee Bar, estimated at approximately 300 g, encompassing the beverage and any accompanying cake or dessert component.

nutrient_source.title: IMR Malaysia MyFCD — Coffee with milk and sugar; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for coffee cake components
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR) MyFCD; USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Shouka Sunrise is a proprietary café item at EC Coffee Bar, Carpenter Street, Singapore. No direct nutritional entry exists in IMR MyFCD, HPB Foodo, or USDA for this specific product. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing: (a) Malaysian-style coffee with milk and sugar (IMR MyFCD reference) and (b) a coffee cake or sponge component (USDA SR Legacy 'Coffee cake, cinnamon with crumb topping, commercially prepared', FDC ID 168869), weighted to reflect a mixed beverage-and-cake serving. Per-100g values represent a blended estimate of the combined item. Confidence is low due to proprietary recipe uncertainty.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Coffee cake / sweet baked goods with coffee analogue
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Shouka Sunrise'. GI estimated at 55 based on analogous sweet coffee-cake items in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al.). Sweet baked goods with coffee and milk components typically fall in the GI range 45–60; 55 is used as a conservative mid-point estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 34.5 × 3.0) / 100 = 56.9 ≈ 57.1 (using 300 g serving, carbohydrate per serving = 103.5 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~95 mg/100 g) and added sugar contributing to caloric load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of Shouka Sunrise due to its moderate sodium content and high sugar load, which can contribute to fluid retention and elevated blood pressure when consumed frequently.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~20 g/100 g) and significant total carbohydrate (~34.5 g/100 g) with a GI of approximately 55
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with Shouka Sunrise as its high sugar and total carbohydrate content, combined with a GI of approximately 55, can produce a substantial glycemic load per serving (~57 GL units), requiring careful carbohydrate budgeting and blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.2 g/100 g), added sugar, and moderate caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should consume Shouka Sunrise sparingly, as its saturated fat and high added-sugar content may adversely affect lipid profiles and contribute to excess caloric intake when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shouka Sunrise is a proprietary café item served at EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street, Singapore, listed under Malaysian cuisine with coffee and cake tags on field survey data. The name 'Shouka' (烧霞, meaning 'burning glow' or 'blazing haze') evokes a sunrise aesthetic, suggesting a visually layered or gradient-coloured beverage or dessert presentation. The item is priced at SGD 17.90, positioning it as a premium café offering. Based on its cuisine tags (Malaysian, Coffee, Cake), it is most likely a coffee-based beverage accompanied by or incorporating a cake or sponge component — a format common in Malaysian-influenced café culture in Singapore. Nutritionally, the estimated per-100g profile reflects a moderately energy-dense item (~220 kcal/100 g) with significant sugar (~20 g/100 g) and moderate fat (~7.5 g/100 g), consistent with a sweetened coffee-cake hybrid. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low category) based on analogous sweet baked goods with coffee components. Clinically, the high sugar and carbohydrate load make this a high-concern item for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, and the saturated fat and sodium content warrant moderation for those managing cardiovascular disease or hypertension. The item is likely halal and vegetarian but contains milk, gluten, and eggs as probable allergens.

Is Shouka Sunrise suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Shouka Sunrise is a proprietary café item with no direct entry in any approved nutritional database (IMR MyFCD, HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by compositing analogous Malaysian coffee-with-milk and coffee cake references. The exact recipe, ingredient ratios, and preparation method are unknown. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: Shouka Sunrise is a proprietary item sold exclusively at EC Coffee Bar, Carpenter Street, Singapore (field survey listing). No direct nutritional data exists in any approved source. All per-100g values are estimated composites based on IMR MyFCD (Malaysian coffee with milk and sugar) and USDA SR Legacy (coffee cake, FDC ID 168869). Vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are reported as -1 because these micronutrients are not reported in the analogous source entries used for estimation and cannot be reliably inferred without direct laboratory analysis. The Chinese names 烧霞日出 (simplified) and 燒霞日出 (traditional) are transliterations/translations of the English name 'Shouka Sunrise' constructed for multilingual coverage; they are not established canonical Chinese names for this product. The Malay, Tamil, and Hindi names are transliterations of the English name. GI set to 55 (low category, ≤55 threshold) based on analogous sweet coffee-cake items from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 57.1 using a 300 g serving size (carbohydrate per serving = 103.5 g; GL = 55 × 103.5 / 100 = 56.9, rounded to 57.1). This record requires laboratory verification before use in clinical or regulatory contexts.
